question: Quoi monitorer et avec quel stack d'observabilite ?

principles.text: Monitorer les 4 golden signals : Latency (P95 < 500ms), Traffic (req/s), Errors (5xx < 0.1%), Saturation (CPU, memoire, connexions DB)

principles: Ajouter le monitoring securite : echecs login, acces non autorises
Definir des SLOs mesurables : disponibilite 99.9%, latence P95 < 500ms, taux d'erreur < 0.1%

java-spring-boot.recommendation: Prometheus (metriques + alerting, CNCF gradue, 67% adoption) + Grafana (dashboards, 43% adoption) + Sentry/GlitchTip (error tracking) + Loki ou ELK (logs) + OpenTelemetry (instrumentation vendor-agnostic). Spring Boot Actuator pour les health endpoints et metriques Micrometer.
sources: SWEBOK v4 chap. 6 — monitoring multi-couche obligatoire (niv. 1)
OWASP A09:2021 — logging des evenements securite (niv. 2)
Google SRE Book chap. 6 — four golden signals (niv. 5)
CNCF — Prometheus et OpenTelemetry projets gradues (niv. 3)
Twelve-Factor App — logs as event streams (niv. 5)

typescript-nestjs.recommendation: Meme stack d'observabilite : Prometheus + Grafana + Sentry/GlitchTip + Loki/ELK + OpenTelemetry. Le stack de monitoring est independant du framework backend.
sources: Google SRE Book chap. 6 — four golden signals (niv. 5)
CNCF — Prometheus et OpenTelemetry projets gradues (niv. 3)

python-django.recommendation: Meme stack d'observabilite : Prometheus + Grafana + Sentry/GlitchTip + Loki/ELK + OpenTelemetry. Le stack de monitoring est independant du framework backend.
sources: Google SRE Book chap. 6 — four golden signals (niv. 5)
